claim: Self-improving AI loops (sensor -> policy -> tools -> quality gate -> learning) that operate autonomously will improve company performance while humans sleep
actionable: Design recursive AI workflows with monitoring, decision rules, tool access, verification, and feedback loops that run unattended and improve iteratively
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI

claim: Companies should optimize for token burn not headcount because AI systems are constrained by token usage, not employee count
actionable: Measure and optimize token usage per employee; prioritize token efficiency over head count reduction in your metrics
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI

claim: Middle management layers are obsolete when AI handles information flow; companies should operate with direct individual contributors and named responsible parties
actionable: Flatten organization structure by replacing middle-management coordination with AI; ensure every action has a named owner (not a committee)
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI

claim: Make all company context legible to AI (recorded, diarized, aggregated) so agents can access institutional knowledge and improve systems autonomously
actionable: Record all meetings, email, Slack, docs in a central system; diarize/summarize into accessible context so agents can learn from company operations
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI

claim: Software artifacts are ephemeral but context/skills/data are valuable; regenerate UI/dashboards monthly as models improve rather than maintaining long-lived code
actionable: Store business context and data long-term but treat generated software (dashboards, internal tools) as disposable; regenerate as models improve
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI

claim: Humans should focus on novel situations, ethical decisions, and high-stakes moments; AI handles routine coordination and optimization
actionable: Reserve human judgment for situations with ethical stakes, novel circumstances, or high emotional intensity; automate everything else
source_title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI
